meta.audit_note: Post-polish re-audit. Original score: 85.5/100. Polished by skill-evaluator@1.0.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated DOIs, PMIDs, or p-values across any output. All thresholds (PP.H4 > 0.8, F > 10, p < 5e-8) are field-standard and correctly applied.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions in any output. Microbiome MR explicitly framed as exploratory across all relevant inputs. No unapproved treatments or clinical recommendations.

methodological_ground.detail: No causal inversion errors. IV validity criteria correctly maintained. Weak-instrument escalation path (WM over IVW when N_IVs < 10) methodologically sound. Ancestry matching and sample overlap addressed in all relevant outputs.

code_usability.detail: R code in Input 5 uses standard TwoSampleMR and MRPRESSO CRAN packages with correct syntax. Inline IV feasibility check (stop() if N_IVs < 3) prevents unrunnable analysis. All GWAS IDs labeled as EXAMPLE IDs with replacement instructions.

inputs.prompt: I want to investigate the causal effect of serum uric acid levels on gout risk. Please design a two-sample MR study plan.

assertions.text: Output contains all four workload configurations (Lite/Standard/Advanced/Publication+)
assertions.note: All four configurations generated with timeframes, modules, strengths, and weaknesses

assertions.text: IV count benchmarks from iv_benchmarks.md are consulted and used to justify the configuration recommendation
assertions.note: Biomarker well-powered class: 10-100 IVs, Very Low risk; used to confirm Standard is appropriate

assertions.text: GWAS database references from gwas_databases.md are cited for both exposure and outcome
assertions.note: UKB for uric acid; FinnGen/UKB for gout; EUR-EUR ancestry matching confirmed

assertions.text: Risk review addresses pleiotropy, ancestry mismatch, and sample overlap
assertions.note: All three reviewer criticisms covered with specific mechanisms and revision strategies

assertions.text: Output distinguishes primary MR evidence, sensitivity support, and higher-tier causal strengthening
assertions.note: Three-tier hierarchy explicitly maintained per Step 6 instructions

inputs.prompt: I need to screen 12 dietary factors (coffee, red meat, fish oil, vegetables, etc.) for causal associations with colorectal cancer. Please generate an MR research plan.

assertions.text: Output contains all four workload configurations
assertions.note: Complete with dietary-specific module descriptions

assertions.text: Per-dietary-factor IV count is explicitly quantified with a weak-instrument risk flag
assertions.note: Dietary class: 2-15 IVs, High risk; communicated clearly; per-factor F-statistic table recommended

assertions.text: Both FDR (BH) and Bonferroni correction are specified for 12 multiple comparisons
assertions.note: Both methods specified; both corrected and uncorrected p-values to be reported

assertions.text: Hard Rule 5 is respected (MVMR not forced unconditionally)
assertions.note: MVMR conditioned on multiple nominally significant dietary exposures

assertions.text: Per-dietary-factor F-statistic reporting is recommended as a supplementary deliverable
assertions.note: Explicitly recommended to pre-empt reviewer challenges on instrument strength

inputs.prompt: I want to study the relationship between sleep duration and depression, but I'm unsure about the direction — it might be bidirectional. Please design an MR plan. Note: I only have two weeks.

assertions.text: Output contains all four workload configurations
assertions.note: Complete

assertions.text: Two-week constraint correctly handled (Lite recommended; bidirectional declined)
assertions.note: Hard Rule 5 applied; escape hatch activated with explicit timeline communication

assertions.text: Escape hatch is activated with explicit communication that bidirectional MR requires 4-8 weeks minimum
assertions.note: Clear, actionable timeline guidance provided

assertions.text: IV count benchmarks for sleep class are consulted (2-30 IVs, Moderate risk)
assertions.note: Sleep duration class correctly looked up; WM preferred over MR-Egger if N_IVs < 20

assertions.text: Weighted Median is recommended as preferred primary estimator when N_IVs is less than 20
assertions.note: Methodologically correct recommendation for low-IV settings

inputs.prompt: Please design a plasma proteome → Alzheimer's disease two-sample MR study. My goal is publication in a high-impact journal.

assertions.text: All four configurations are generated and complete
assertions.note: Complete; Publication+ correctly recommended for high-impact target

assertions.text: cis-pQTL priority is enforced with the ±1 Mb extraction window explicitly specified
assertions.note: Correct cis definition; trans-pQTL flagged as high pleiotropy risk

assertions.text: Colocalization is required with PP.H4 > 0.8 threshold stated
assertions.note: Standard threshold correctly specified; HyPrColoc/coloc mentioned as tools

assertions.text: IV count benchmarks for the protein cis-pQTL class are consulted (1-10 IVs, Moderate risk)
assertions.note: Correctly looked up; implications for instrument strategy clearly drawn

assertions.text: Sample overlap between the pQTL GWAS source and the AD GWAS is explicitly addressed
assertions.note: UKB proteome vs UKB-derived AD GWAS overlap check recommended

inputs.prompt: I want to use MR to study the causal effects of gut microbiome diversity on three outcomes — type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and depression — with bidirectional validation. Please give me a complete study plan and an R code framework.

assertions.text: All four workload configurations are present
assertions.note: Complete; Advanced-Publication+ correctly recommended

assertions.text: Microbiome IV count benchmarks are consulted (1-5 IVs, Very High risk) and communicated to the user
assertions.note: MiBioGen typical range stated; feasibility assessment required before committing; exploratory framing mandatory

assertions.text: All GWAS IDs in the R code framework are labeled 'EXAMPLE ID — replace before running'
assertions.note: Every GWAS ID in the code block carries the EXAMPLE ID comment; no unlabeled IDs remain

assertions.text: An inline IV feasibility check (stop if N_IVs < 3) is included in the R code
assertions.note: stop() condition added immediately after extract_instruments(); prevents silently running infeasible MR

assertions.text: Exploratory framing is enforced throughout the output; publication skepticism is explicitly acknowledged
assertions.note: Both in-text and escape hatch language frame results as hypothesis generation only

key_strengths: Perfect assertion pass rate (25/25) after polishing — the iv_benchmarks.md and gwas_databases.md reference files resolved all previously failing assertions by providing structured, class-specific guidance accessible at execution time.

Progressive disclosure now fully implemented: SKILL.md at 152 lines with two cleanly scoped reference files; GWAS tables and IV benchmarks can be updated independently without touching core workflow logic.
Fault tolerance for instrument scarcity is now explicit: three failure modes (GWAS unavailable, N_IVs < 3, F < 10) have defined response procedures, raising Reliability from 6/12 to 10/12.
R Code Framework Guidelines section with mandatory EXAMPLE ID comments and inline IV feasibility checks eliminates the code-misuse risk identified in the original audit.

Input Validation section and Hard Rule 11 together provide comprehensive boundary enforcement: vague inputs trigger default-inference, out-of-scope inputs trigger polite redirect, infeasible analyses trigger explicit warnings.

recommendations.title: Add a formal output schema for the per-configuration summary block
recommendations.problem: The four-configuration table output format is described in prose but not as a formal template, leading to slight structural variation across complex inputs.
recommendations.root_cause: Step 2 describes what to include per configuration but does not provide a fixed markdown template for the output block.
recommendations.fix: Add a fixed output template to Step 2 or a new references/output_templates.md showing the exact markdown table format expected for each configuration block, ensuring structural consistency across all inputs.

recommendations.title: Add example test input / expected output pair to support testability
recommendations.problem: The skill has no documented example test input with expected output, making automated regression testing difficult if the skill is updated in future.
recommendations.root_cause: Testability (Static Category 7.3) scored 3/4 due to absence of example test cases despite clear parameter thresholds.
recommendations.fix: Add a ## Usage Examples section at the end of SKILL.md with one canonical example (e.g., uric acid → gout) showing the expected structure of the four-configuration output. This also improves discoverability for new users.
